info.title: Ontology Platform MVP6.10 Multi-tenant Planning API
info.description: Planning-only OpenAPI artifact for MVP6.10 Multi-tenant Runtime P0 (read-only tenant context + strict isolation). Additive to MVP1-MVP6.9; replaces no prior OpenAPI draft. The surface is READ-ONLY: it lists the tenants the current actor is an ACTIVE member of ('my tenants'), returns a single tenant summary, lists a tenant's projects (tenant-scoped), and resolves the tenant a project lives in. Nothing is provisioned, mutated, or re-homed: no tenant create/update/delete, no membership mutation, no cross-tenant read, no tenant_id column / FK / migration. Tenant->project mapping is a deterministic fixture. STRICT ISOLATION is the headline invariant (default-deny, 404-not-leak): the caller's visibility set is exactly the tenants where they have an ACTIVE TenantMembership on a non-ARCHIVED tenant; a tenant not in the set (unknown / ARCHIVED / not-a-member) -> 404 TENANT_NOT_FOUND (existence NEVER leaked, 404 not 403); a known-but-inactive relationship (membership SUSPENDED or tenant SUSPENDED) -> 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED; .../tenants/{A}/projects never returns another tenant's projects; /projects/{id}/tenant for an out-of-visibility project -> 404 PROJECT_NOT_FOUND. Every response carries an all-false 8-flag TenantMutationGuard. Reuses MVP5 Role + dev-auth actor and MVP1 project shapes by reference (no renames). See ADR 0017 and docs/pm/MVP6_10_MULTI_TENANT_BRIEF.md. All example values are non-sensitive placeholders.

tags.name: MVP6.10 Multi-tenant
tags.description: Read-only tenant context + strict isolation. Nothing provisioned, mutated, or re-homed; visibility set = ACTIVE membership on non-ARCHIVED tenant; default-deny 404-not-leak; all-false 8-flag mutation guard on every response.

get.description: Read-only list of EXACTLY the tenants in the current actor's visibility set: tenants where the actor has an ACTIVE TenantMembership on a non-ARCHIVED tenant. Never returns any other tenant's id/name/count/summary; a tenant the actor is not an ACTIVE member of, or that is ARCHIVED, never appears (existence not leaked). Deterministic over the fixture. Carries an all-false TenantMutationGuard. actor_id is an OPTIONAL dev-only override for negative/cross-tenant tests (open gate G1); absent -> the MVP5 dev-auth actor.

200.description: The actor's visibility set (may be empty)

get.description: Read-only summary of a single tenant the actor is an ACTIVE member of. Isolation: tenant_id NOT in the visibility set (unknown / ARCHIVED / not-a-member) -> 404 TENANT_NOT_FOUND (existence never leaked -> 404, never 403); a known-but-inactive relationship (membership SUSPENDED or tenant SUSPENDED) -> 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED. Any ACTIVE member (even Role=VIEWER) may read; Role is surfaced for future write/admin surfaces but not required for this read. Carries an all-false TenantMutationGuard.

Read-only, tenant-scoped list of the projects owned by tenant_id, via the deterministic fixture tenant->project mapping. Isolation: returns ONLY projects whose owning tenant is tenant_id; a project belonging to another tenant is NEVER returned here, even for an actor who is a member of tenant_id (cross_tenant_access_granted stays false). Same 404/403 isolation rules as the tenant summary. Project items reuse the MVP1 project summary shape by reference (no rename); existing MVP1-MVP6.9 per-project endpoints are unchanged and tenant-unaware. Carries an all-false TenantMutationGuard.

200.description: Projects owned by this tenant (may be empty)

get.description: Read-only resolution of the tenant a given project logically lives in, via the deterministic fixture mapping (no tenant_id column / FK). Isolation: if the project's owning tenant is NOT in the caller's visibility set -> 404 PROJECT_NOT_FOUND (the project's existence is not leaked across the tenant boundary); an unknown project_id likewise -> 404 PROJECT_NOT_FOUND. A known-but-inactive owning-tenant relationship -> 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED. This is the trimmable surface (open gate G4; default: keep). Carries an all-false TenantMutationGuard.

200.description: The tenant the project lives in

TenantIdPath.name: tenant_id
TenantIdPath.description: Target tenant id. Not in the caller's visibility set -> 404 TENANT_NOT_FOUND (existence not leaked).

ProjectIdPath.name: project_id
ProjectIdPath.description: Target project id (reuses the MVP1 project id space by reference). Out-of-visibility or unknown -> 404 PROJECT_NOT_FOUND.

ActorIdQuery.name: actor_id
ActorIdQuery.description: OPTIONAL dev-only actor override so QA can be 'the actor who is / is not a member' for negative/cross-tenant tests (mirrors the MVP6.5 governance pattern). Absent -> the MVP5 dev-auth actor. Open gate G1; exact mechanism finalized in Wave52. Never a real auth/JWT tenant claim.

TenantNotFound.description: Tenant not in the caller's visibility set: unknown, ARCHIVED, or not-a-member. Returns 404 TENANT_NOT_FOUND (NEVER 403) so tenant existence is not leaked. Body carries no other tenant's id/name/count.

ProjectNotFound.description: Project unknown, or its owning tenant is out of the caller's visibility set. Returns 404 PROJECT_NOT_FOUND so cross-tenant project existence is not leaked.

AccessSuspended.description: The caller has a KNOWN relationship to the tenant but it is inactive (membership SUSPENDED or tenant SUSPENDED). Returns 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED (safe: the caller already knows the tenant exists). Distinct from 403 PERMISSION_DENIED, which is reserved for future role-gated writes and is not produced by P0 reads.

TenantStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a tenant (frozen). ACTIVE: readable by ACTIVE members. SUSPENDED: known-but-inactive -> 403 for members. ARCHIVED: treated as out-of-visibility -> 404 (existence not leaked). No mutation in P0; deterministic fixture value.

TenantMembershipStatus.description: Status of an (actor, tenant) membership (frozen). ACTIVE: contributes the tenant to the actor's visibility set. SUSPENDED: known-but-inactive relationship -> 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED.

TenantAccessDenialReason.description: Response-side isolation taxonomy explaining a denial. NOT_A_MEMBER -> 404 TENANT_NOT_FOUND; TENANT_ARCHIVED -> 404 TENANT_NOT_FOUND (existence not leaked); MEMBERSHIP_SUSPENDED -> 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED; TENANT_SUSPENDED -> 403 TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED. Carried in ApiError.details.denial_reason on isolation errors.

Role.description: MVP5 RBAC Role reused VERBATIM (no new literal); defined locally to keep this draft self-contained (same names as apps/backend/app/core/enums.py). Carried on a TenantMembership and surfaced for future write/admin surfaces; P0 reads require only an ACTIVE membership (even VIEWER may read).

TenantMutationGuard.description: All-false mutation guard present on EVERY tenant response (my-tenants list, tenant summary, tenant projects, project->tenant). Every flag is false, always; MVP6.10 turns NO flag true, ever. Mirrors the MVP6.1-6.5/6.7/6.9 all-false pattern. cross_tenant_access_granted and project_rehomed are the isolation-specific assertions QA leans on hardest.

TenantMembership.description: An (actor_id, tenant_id, role, status) relationship. role reuses MVP5 Role verbatim; status is TenantMembershipStatus. Read-only fixture; never created/removed/role-changed in P0 (membership_mutated stays false). Surfaced on a TenantSummary as my_membership so the caller's Role + membership status are visible to future write/admin surfaces.

TenantSummary.description: Read-only summary of one tenant in the caller's visibility set. Shared item shape for the my-tenants list, the single-tenant summary, and the project->tenant resolution. Carries the caller's own membership (my_membership) so Role + status are surfaced; NEVER carries another actor's membership or another tenant's data.

status.description: Always ACTIVE for a tenant that appears in a visibility-set response (SUSPENDED -> 403, ARCHIVED -> 404, so neither surfaces here).

project_count.description: Exact count of projects owned by this tenant via the fixture mapping (scoped to this tenant only).

ProjectSummaryRef.description: Project summary reused BY REFERENCE from the MVP1 ProjectSummary (same field names; defined locally to keep this draft self-contained, NO rename). The MVP1 Project model is unchanged and tenant-unaware; this is a read overlay, not a schema change (no tenant_id field is added to the project).

status.description: MVP1 ProjectStatus reused by reference (DRAFT/ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/DELETED). Not re-declared as an enum here to avoid duplicating the MVP1 definition.

TenantListResponse.description: The actor's visibility set (my tenants). items contains EXACTLY the tenants the actor is an ACTIVE member of on a non-ARCHIVED tenant; never any other tenant. May be empty.

actor_id.description: The resolved actor whose visibility set this is (dev-auth actor, or the actor_id override).

total_count.description: Exact size of the visibility set (== items length; the list is never truncated in P0).

TenantProjectListResponse.description: Tenant-scoped project list. items contains ONLY projects owned by tenant_id via the fixture mapping; another tenant's project is never returned here.

total_count.description: Exact count of projects owned by this tenant (== items length in P0).

ProjectTenantResponse.description: Resolves the tenant a project logically lives in (fixture mapping). Only succeeds when the owning tenant is in the caller's visibility set; otherwise 404 PROJECT_NOT_FOUND.

ApiError.description: Standard error envelope. P0 tenant isolation codes: TENANT_NOT_FOUND (404, existence not leaked), TENANT_ACCESS_SUSPENDED (403, known-but-inactive), PROJECT_NOT_FOUND (404, out-of-visibility or unknown project). details.denial_reason (when present) is a TenantAccessDenialReason. 403 PERMISSION_DENIED is reserved for future role-gated writes and is NOT produced by P0 reads.

items.description: Second tenant the actor also belongs to (mock).

value.message: 해당 테넌트에 대한 접근이 일시 중지되었습니다.
